text: Literally, ‘the angel who spoke in (or, by means of) me’. The Hebrew text can also be rendered, ‘the angel who spoke to me’ (cf. Num. 12.6 and 8). But see note on 2.3.
The horns evidently represent four (or perhaps two) enemies of the Jewish people, but they cannot be identified with certainty.

‘Turn them back’; literally, ‘scare them away’. It seems probable that there is an error in the manuscripts, and that some word more appropriate to the activities of blacksmiths originally appeared there.
